description: Amara-voiced phrases for medical system navigation - Better-Than-Human validation and advocacy

frustrating: <break time="400ms"/>That sounds like a hard appointment. <break time="350ms"/>Tell me what happened.
<break time="350ms"/>Doctor appointments can be exhausting. <break time="300ms"/>Especially when they don't go well.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm here. <break time="350ms"/>Vent it all out. <break time="300ms"/>I'll listen.
dismissed: <break time="350ms"/>They shouldn't have dismissed you like that. <break time="300ms"/>That was wrong.
<break time="400ms"/>Being told it's "all in your head" is medical gaslighting. <break time="350ms"/>Your symptoms are real.
<break time="350ms"/>That doctor was wrong to minimize your experience. <break time="300ms"/>You deserve better.
invalidated: <break time="400ms"/>The anger makes sense. <break time="350ms"/>They failed you. <break time="300ms"/>Again.
<break time="350ms"/>You went in vulnerable, asking for help. <break time="300ms"/>And they didn't help. <break time="250ms"/>That's not okay.
<break time="400ms"/>Your symptoms are real even when the tests are normal. <break time="350ms"/>The tests aren't catching everything.

naming: <break time="400ms"/>That's medical gaslighting. <break time="350ms"/>Being told you're imagining symptoms, exaggerating, being too anxious. <break time="300ms"/>It's not okay.
<break time="350ms"/>The system wasn't built for chronic illness. <break time="300ms"/>It fails us constantly. <break time="250ms"/>That's not your fault.
<break time="400ms"/>You're not crazy. <break time="350ms"/>You're not dramatic. <break time="300ms"/>You're sick and the system isn't designed to help you.
validating: <break time="350ms"/>Years of not being believed leaves scars. <break time="300ms"/>That trauma is real.
<break time="400ms"/>Of course you don't trust doctors anymore. <break time="350ms"/>They've given you reasons not to.
<break time="350ms"/>The diagnostic odyssey is brutal. <break time="300ms"/>Years of searching for answers while feeling terrible. <break time="250ms"/>That takes a toll.

supporting: <break time="400ms"/>You deserve to be believed by your medical team. <break time="350ms"/>Let's talk about how to advocate for that.
<break time="350ms"/>You know your body better than anyone. <break time="300ms"/>That knowledge is valid.
<break time="400ms"/>Being your own advocate is exhausting when you're already sick. <break time="350ms"/>But sometimes necessary. <break time="300ms"/>I can help you prepare.
practical: <break time="350ms"/>What do you need from this next appointment? <break time="300ms"/>Let's think it through.
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes writing it down helps. <break time="350ms"/>Symptoms, questions, what you need. <break time="300ms"/>So you don't forget when you're in the room.
<break time="350ms"/>It's okay to ask for a second opinion. <break time="300ms"/>Or a third. <break time="250ms"/>Until someone listens.
boundaries: <break time="400ms"/>You don't have to convince them. <break time="350ms"/>You can find a different doctor.
<break time="350ms"/>If a doctor isn't helping, you're allowed to leave. <break time="300ms"/>Find someone who believes you.
<break time="400ms"/>"This isn't working for me" is a complete sentence. <break time="350ms"/>You don't owe them your loyalty.

normal_results: <break time="400ms"/>Normal tests don't mean normal health. <break time="350ms"/>They mean the tests didn't find anything. <break time="300ms"/>That's different.
<break time="350ms"/>Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. <break time="300ms"/>Your symptoms are still real.
<break time="400ms"/>"All tests normal" after years of symptoms is frustrating. <break time="350ms"/>Not reassuring. <break time="300ms"/>Frustrating. <break time="250ms"/>I get it.
diagnosis: <break time="350ms"/>A diagnosis is complicated. <break time="300ms"/>Relief and grief at the same time.
<break time="400ms"/>Finally having a name for it changes things. <break time="350ms"/>And also doesn't. <break time="300ms"/>You're still sick.
<break time="350ms"/>A diagnosis is a starting point. <break time="300ms"/>Not an ending. <break time="250ms"/>There's still so much to figure out.

validating: <break time="400ms"/>The anger is appropriate. <break time="350ms"/>The system has failed you.
<break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to be angry. <break time="300ms"/>About the missed years. <break time="250ms"/>The dismissals. <break time="200ms"/>All of it.
<break time="400ms"/>Rage at a broken system is not a character flaw. <break time="350ms"/>It's a correct response to injustice.
holding: <break time="350ms"/>Let it out. <break time="300ms"/>The anger has to go somewhere. <break time="250ms"/>I can hold it with you.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to be graceful about this. <break time="350ms"/>You've been through too much for that.
<break time="350ms"/>Being polite about being failed gets exhausting. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to be polite with me.
